title: Feature Req: All images / all images since
body:
I've been using Skopeo for a while and I believe there's a few QOL and feature improvements that could be made. Excuse me if any of these are out of bounds. 

- While syncing images, the ability to choose all images would be extremely helpful for mirroring.
- The ability to additionally scope via manifest "created" for things like "all tags within the past 90 days"

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

> * While syncing images, the ability to choose all images would be extremely helpful for mirroring.

What does “All images” mean,  from what universe?

> * The ability to quantify the most used fsLayers and pull them first.

For what benefit? AFAICS either the whole image is available, or not; pulling a frequently-used partial image part doesn’t help any image to be pulled faster.

> * The functionality of multithreading for multiple pulls at once. Today I use a loop of "docker run skopeo sync" across multiple YAML configs.

Worth considering; the individual copies are already copying layers by parallel, it’s not trivially clear to me how wide the parallelism can go to still be beneficial.

All images example: I want to mirror the entirety of  registry.access.redhat.com,  quay.io, etc
With the ability to specifically pull all tags created in the past 90 days. I see this as an alternative to images and images-by-tag-regex. 
We can scope this back to those 2 specific features. I'll redact the rest. 

`registry.example.com:
    images:
        busybox: []
        redis:
            - "1.0"
            - "2.0"
            - "sha256:0000000000000000000000000000000011111111111111111111111111111111"
    images-by-tag-regex:
        nginx: ^1\.13\.[12]-alpine-perl$

registry.access.redhat.com:
  images:
    *: [] # for all tags OR

registry.access.redhat.com:
  images-since-date: 
    *:  01-01-2021
`

> All images example: I want to mirror the entirety of registry.access.redhat.com, quay.io, etc

I’m afraid that isn’t possible; there isn’t a “list everything” API reliably available across the various registries, in particular `r.a.rh.com` does not provide it. (And personally I would say that mirroring a whole public registry, or even examining every single image on a public registry for the last build date, is not something that I feel very comfortable offering with a fairly naive solution like `skopeo sync`, anyway. Of course if it’s a software that can be built, not offering it is not going to stop a motivated user, but it should not be something anyone tries to do just because it is listed in a man page.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
